title: Class Methods
content:
### Types of Methods

Python has three types of methods:

---

### Instance Methods

Regular methods that work on an instance (have `self`):

```python
class Person:
    def __init__(self, name):
        self.name = name
    
    def greet(self):  # Instance method
        return f"Hello, {self.name}"
```

---

### Class Methods (`@classmethod`)

Work on the class itself (have `cls`):

```python
class Person:
    count = 0
    
    def __init__(self, name):
        self.name = name
        Person.count += 1
    
    @classmethod
    def total_people(cls):
        return f"Total: {cls.count}"

print(Person.total_people())  # Total: 0
p1 = Person("Alice")
print(Person.total_people())  # Total: 1
```

---

### Static Methods (`@staticmethod`)

Do not have access to instance or class:

```python
class Calculator:
    @staticmethod
    def add(a, b):
        return a + b
    
    @staticmethod
    def multiply(a, b):
        return a * b

print(Calculator.add(5, 3))       # 8
print(Calculator.multiply(5, 3))  # 15
```

---

### Comparison

| Method Type | First Param | Access |
|-------------|-------------|--------|
| Instance | `self` | Instance + class |
| Class | `cls` | Class only |
| Static | None | Nothing (utility) |

---

### Class Method as Alternative Constructor

```python
class Person:
    def __init__(self, name, age):
        self.name = name
        self.age = age
    
    @classmethod
    def from_birth_year(cls, name, year):
        return cls(name, 2024 - year)

p = Person.from_birth_year("Alice", 1999)
print(p.age)  # 25
```

> **Best Practice:** Use instance methods for normal behavior, class methods for factory methods, static methods for utility functions related to the class.
